statements.name: PROC CAUSALGRAPH
statements.help:
PROC CAUSALGRAPH <options>;                 
	IDENTIFY  effect-specification;                 

	MODEL  'label' path <, path...> < / options >;                 

	PATHDIAGRAM <options>;                 

	TESTID <'label'> variable-list </ options>;                 

	UNMEASURED  variables;

arguments.name: COMMON
arguments.description: requests adjustment sets common to all models

arguments.name: COMPACT
arguments.description: suppresses the display of unused variables in a table

arguments.name: CYCLES
arguments.description: displays directed cycles in each model that you specify by using a MODEL statement

arguments.name: DISCONNECTED
arguments.description: suppresses warnings for models that contain disjoint directed acyclic graphs (DAGs)

arguments.name: IMAP
arguments.description: requests a list of the conditional independence properties (an independence map) that are encoded by each causal model

arguments.name: INMODEL=
arguments.description: specifies a data set that contains one or more causal models to be analyzed

arguments.name: LIST
arguments.description: requests the possible identification criteria for each model

arguments.name: MAXLIST=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of identification criteria to be printed when you specify the LIST option

arguments.name: MAXSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum size of the adjustment sets to be constructed when you specify the LIST option

arguments.name: METHOD=
arguments.description: specifies the method to use for identifying a causal effect
arguments.help: METHOD=ADJUSTMENT | ANCESTOR | BACKDOOR | POUTCOME | PTREATMENT | IV

arguments.name: MINIMAL
arguments.description: requests a list of only minimal adjustment sets when you specify the LIST option

arguments.name: NOLIST
arguments.description: excludes the construction of possible identification criteria

arguments.name: NOPRINT
arguments.description: suppresses all displayed output

arguments.name: NOSORT
arguments.description: requests an unsorted list of adjustment sets when METHOD=ADJUSTMENT or BACKDOOR

arguments.name: NTHREADS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of simultaneous computational threads available to the procedure

arguments.name: ORDERMODELS
arguments.description: prints the model results in alphabetical order by model label

arguments.name: OUTMODEL=
arguments.description: creates an output data set that contains the causal model specifications

arguments.name: PSUMMARY
arguments.description: displays only the summary of the causal effect identification analysis

statements.name: IDENTIFY
statements.description: specifies the causal effects to be analyzed

statements.name: MODEL
statements.description: A MODEL statement specifies a causal model in the form of a directed acyclic graph (DAG)

arguments.name: NOANALYSIS
arguments.description: excludes the model from the identification analysis

arguments.name: REFMODEL=
arguments.description: specifies a reference model to use as a starting point for the current model

arguments.name: REMOVE=
arguments.description: specifies paths to remove after the reference model has been processed

arguments.name: REMOVENODES=
arguments.description: specifies one or more nodes to remove after PROC CAUSALGRAPH processes the reference model

statements.name: PATHDIAGRAM
statements.description: requests a path diagram, which is a visual representation of a graphical causal model

arguments.name: ARRANGE=
arguments.description: specifies the algorithm for laying out the variables in the path diagram

arguments.name: DESIGNHEIGHT=
arguments.description: sets the height of the path diagram, in number of pixels, where h is between 100 and 10,922

arguments.name: DESIGNWIDTH=
arguments.description: sets the width of the path diagram, in number of pixels, where w is between 100 and 10,922

arguments.name: DIAGRAMLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies the label of the path diagram

arguments.name: LABEL=
arguments.description: specifies the labels of variables to be displayed in path diagrams, where each varlabel has the following form: variable=label You can use any valid SAS names or quoted strings of up to 256 characters for labels

arguments.name: MODEL=
arguments.description: requests path diagrams for a list of models, which are specified by their associated labels

arguments.name: NOTITLE
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the default title

arguments.name: SCALE=
arguments.description: specifies the scaling factor, n, for the node size relative to the dimensions of the path diagram

arguments.name: TEXTSIZEMIN=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum text size, i, in the output path diagram

arguments.name: TITLE=
arguments.description: specifies the title of the path diagram

statements.name: TESTID
statements.description: tests whether a criterion that you specify is valid for identifying a causal effect

arguments.name: CONDITIONAL=
arguments.description: performs a test for a conditional instrumental variable, where the variables or variable is a list of conditioning variables

arguments.name: PATHS
arguments.description: creates an enumeration of the proper paths from the treatment variables to the outcome variables in each model

statements.name: UNMEASURED
statements.description: specifies a list of variables that are treated as unmeasured
